source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A warm, restrained Japandi interior that balances Japanese minimalism with Scandinavian warmth — natural materials, low profiles, and layered soft neutrals. Rooms should feel airy and meditative but family-friendly: tactile rugs, rounded wooden forms, and purposeful storage. Introduce a compact work corner and a safe crib placement in the bedroom while preserving open sightlines to the patio and natural light.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals anchored by pale off-white and sandy beiges, layered with honey oak and muted mid-browns for wood grain emphasis. Deep charcoal used sparingly for contrast (legs, small fixtures). Greens from plants are accents only. This keeps scenes calm on camera while revealing texture and grain.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, warm ambient light with diffused daylight from large living/dining openings. Accent with warm (2700–3000K) local fixtures: woven rattan pendant over the dining table, low floor/side lamps near seating and tea nook, and a dimmable desk lamp in the work corner. Avoid harsh overhead fluorescents; prefer directional warm spots for focal points.
materials: warm oak (tables, low sofa bases, shelving)
brushed brass/bronze in small accents (sconces, pulls)
furniture_language: low-profile seating with exposed wooden bases
round dining table to soften corners
clean, minimal shelving with closed storage for toys
small, slim-profile standing desk — vertical grain wood or white matte top
compact crib with simple slatted form and rounded edges
styling_rules: Keep horizontal sightlines and negative space; avoid tall, visual cluttering pieces near main openings.
Use one focal texture per scene (e.g., rattan pendant in dining, tatami-edge rug in living).
Limit prints and art to 1–2 small, framed natural studies per room; prefer sculptural ceramics and plants as decor.
Scale furniture to measured room sizes — choose two-seat sofas/compact sectional for Nest (≈11.9' × 11.7').
Design a dedicated work corner under soft daylight with an unobtrusive standing desk and a task lamp; avoid isolating from family sightlines.
avoid: overstuffed high-back furniture that blocks sightlines
excessive small decorative objects or busy patterns
open shelving overflowing with items — keep storage closed or minimal

room_guidance.headline: Low-profile family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Place a low two-seater sofa (wood base) oriented toward the patio glazing to emphasize indoor-outdoor flow. Anchor with a low wooden coffee table and layered natural-fibre rug with subtle tatami edge detail. Locate a slim standing desk in the brighter corner by the patio/large glass door so the occupant faces the room. Include closed storage benches for toys along an adjacent wall.
must_include: low two-seat sofa on wooden base (scale for 11.9' × 11.7')
layered neutral rug with tatami edge
one medium potted plant (e.g., ficus or large philodendron)

room_guidance.headline: Calm bedroom with safe crib and cozy adult zone
room_guidance.guidance: Place a low-profile bed on the longest wall; keep headboard minimal and tactile. Position the crib (compact, slatted) on a short wall near the bedroom window but away from direct drafts and radiators; ensure flow to the closet. Add a small reading nook with a soft lounge chair or floor cushion and a slim bedside table. Use layered textiles and a small rug to ground the crib area.
must_include: small textured area rug by bed
must_avoid: crib directly under window or blocking closet door
tall bulky wardrobes that dominate the room

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Use a round oak table sized for the small bay (≈3.5'–4' diameter) with 3–4 light linen-upholstered chairs. Suspend a woven rattan pendant centered over the table at a warm glow. Keep table styling minimal: one textured stoneware vase and two neutral place settings. Maintain clear circulation to kitchen and patio.
must_avoid: overly bright pendant or multiple competing fixtures

room_guidance.headline: Compact, muted kitchen — functional and minimal
room_guidance.guidance: Keep cabinetry flat-fronted in a warm neutral with matte finish; use open counter space for a single ceramic bowl and a small potted herb. If visible, stage a single barstool at a peninsula. Lighting should be soft undercabinet and warm overhead; avoid heavy commercial appliances on surfaces.

room_guidance.headline: Covered outdoor extension for slow mornings
room_guidance.guidance: Stage with low seating (bench or floor cushions), a small teak/wood side table, and a woven lantern. Keep planting simple (two large planters) and allow daylight to be prominent in the frame. Use the patio as a framed extension of the living/dining interiors.

warnings: Many secondary rooms lack measured dimensions — verify furniture scale before final staging.
Crib placement must respect local safety codes and away from windows; this plan assumes safe clearance.
Standing desk shown near glazing assumes access to electrical outlet; confirm during set staging.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm, warm minimalism — quietly textured and family-friendly.
global_style_notes: Favor horizontal lines and low profiles to emphasize Japanese influence.
Layer three neutral textures per scene (wood + textile + ceramic) for camera depth.
Keep an uncluttered foreground; store family items in closed benches or low cabinets.
Use plants selectively as living accents — one medium plant per main space.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: No bright saturated colors, no glossy chrome/reflective metallics, no heavy Victorian or ornate detailing, no cluttered open shelving, no high-back bulky seating, no childrens' bright toys left in frame.

Rooms should feel airy and meditative but family-friendly: tactile rugs, rounded wooden forms, and purposeful storage. Introduce a compact work corner and a safe crib placement in the bedroom while preserving open sightlines to the patio and natural light.
